metadata.methodology: Specification by Example (Gojko Adzic) - Living Documentation

top_level_structure.description: Root folder for all executable specifications and living documentation

children.purpose: Table of contents, navigation guide, and glossary index
Ubiquitous language from Chris-language.json with status markers (agreed/proposed/unverified)
K1-K8 refusal detectors and cross-references (GOAL and REQ mapping)

step_definitions: Step 5: Joint Panel Open & Role Allocation
Step 6: Deep-Dive Technical Reviews (SORA, Safety, Autonomy, ML)
Step 9: Trial Archive & Forensic Data Preservation

children.purpose: Features spanning multiple phases (classification, apportionment, audit, accessibility)

children.description: REQ018: MOD classified evidence handling per CAP 722H/JSP 440
REQ027: Clause-to-evidence binding and audit logging
Cross-cutting accessibility for CAA officers and investigators

children.purpose: Step definitions, test fixtures, and architecture notes

children.description: Gherkin step definitions (Given/When/Then) keyed to Behave Python
Test data factory definitions (applicants, aircraft, evidence packs)
Technical architecture patterns (Artefact Detector, Clause Mapper, Decision Engine)

tags.description: Safety-critical or authorization-blocking features (containment, termination, meaningful human control)
High-impact features affecting case workflow (intake, SORA, decision logging)
Nice-to-have or internal-only features (archival, calibration feedback)
Fastest path through system: complete pack → acceptance → SORA → approval
Broader coverage: error paths, edge cases, rework cycles
Complete coverage including slow paths (classification, MAA coordination, appeals)
Geofence, C2 link, termination, and flight safety
Swarm decision-making, emergent behavior, ML assurance
Meaningful human control, workload, crew takeover
Data recording, MOR handling, forensic investigation
PackCompleteness: Mandatory artefacts missing or insufficient
GeofenceContainment: Boundary tolerance or fail-safe undefined
MeaningfulHumanControl: Crew authority compromised or unverifiable
MLForensicExplainability: ML model not explainable or frozen

navigation_aids.navigation_strategy: Phase-first (Phase 1→2→3) with cross-cutting domain index (classification, apportionment, audit)

persona_navigation_paths.question: How do I add a new feature to the system?
What does the system do when classified evidence is uploaded?
What evidence do I need for REQ009 ML assurance?
I need to reconstruct the decision-making around Case #123

inconsistent_usage: BVLOS: Sometimes 'BVLOS', sometimes 'Beyond Visual Line of Sight' (expand acronym consistently on first use)
swarm-aware: Used in GOAL-002, SORA specs, but definition still 'proposed' in glossary
meaningfulhumancontrol: Hyphenated in glossary (meaningful-human-control) but sometimes written as 3 words in examples
glossary_terms_in_specs.recommendation: Add consistency check pre-commit hook: all @REQ tags must use glossary terms

over_specified: RSPEC-010 (SwarmAwareSORA) — 47-line given/when/then with 8 sub-steps; break into separate scenarios
under_specified: Step 9 Archive (phase3) — only 1 feature file; need specs for: data retention duration, access control, AAIB extraction, breach notification
Step 10 Calibration — no specs; examples file mentions but features missing

duplicate_or_near_duplicate.recommendation: Consolidate: EX-001 should be a worked example linked from RSPEC-002, not a separate spec

missing_goal_preamble: step4_readiness_check features lack Goal/Why statement linking to GOAL-001
step7_deliberation_and_override features lack Goal/Why statement linking to GOAL-015

missing_cross_references.should_reference: ml_assurance (step 6, ML decisions must be overridable)

criteria.criterion: New developer can find architecture patterns without prior context
criteria.assessment: MEDIUM — features/support/architecture_notes.md exists but only 200 lines; needs expansion with diagrams

criteria.criterion: CAA case officer can read Gherkin without programming background
criteria.assessment: HIGH — Feature files use plain English (Given/When/Then); glossary provided for jargon (SORA, SAIL, ODD, etc.)

criteria.criterion: Applicant engineer can find evidence requirements by REQ code
criteria.assessment: HIGH — @REQ tags on all features; README provides index; cross-cutting classification folder is discoverable

criteria.criterion: AAIB investigator can reconstruct audit trail from docs
criteria.assessment: MEDIUM — audit_and_traceability folder exists; but forensic data format not documented in living docs (reference to Vibe meters needed)

accessibility_assessment.lowest_scoring_persona: AAIB Investigator (due to forensic data format documentation gap)
improvement_actions: Add 'Forensic Data Format' reference page in cross_cutting/audit_and_traceability/
Add diagrams to support/architecture_notes.md showing data flow and audit trail structure
Expand step9_archive/ with concrete examples of Vibe meter outputs

tooling.rationale: Relish natively supports Behave, provides web interface for non-developers, auto-publishes on feature file changes
alternatives: Spectacle (SpectacleForBDD.com) — better PDF export, less web UI
Living Doc (Living Doc by Cucumber) — newer, but Cucumber-centric (project uses Behave)
Pickles (PicklesDoc.github.io) — mature, good filtering, but requires manual HTML build

sync_process.description: Keep executable specs in sync with regulatory clause library

pre_commit_hook.description: Fail commit if feature file references a deprecated clause or undefined glossary term
pre_commit_hook.implementation: Python script checking @REQ tags against clause-library-version.json

nightly_build.description: Auto-republish living docs on feature file changes; archive old versions
nightly_build.implementation: GitHub Actions workflow: on push to sbe-output/features/ → run Relish build → publish to GitHub Pages

quarterly_audit.description: Review which specs haven't run in 30+ days; mark for removal or update
quarterly_audit.implementation: Query Cypress test run logs; generate drift report; send to product team

versioning.strategy: Pin clause-library version to each decision record
versioning.mechanism: Each DECISION_RECORD contains clause-library-version=vN.M; old versions archived in /archives/v1.0/, /archives/v1.1/, etc.
process: When clause library updates, increment version and re-publish all specs
In-flight cases continue under pinned version; new cases use current version
Archive system allows AAIB to reconstruct what was known at time of decision

drift_audit.query: SELECT feature_file, last_test_run_date FROM test_metrics WHERE last_test_run_date < NOW() - interval '30 days'
drift_audit.candidates_for_removal: Specs that have not run in 90+ days; candidates for archival in /deprecated/
drift_audit.candidates_for_update: Specs referencing deprecated terms or outdated REQ codes
drift_audit.reporting: Dashboard showing test coverage heatmap per phase per goal
